learning_objectives.text: Define diabetes mellitus types, metabolic syndrome components, and key epidemiological terms related to diabetes burden globally
Explain the pathophysiology of Type 1, Type 2, and gestational diabetes, and describe risk factors contributing to the global diabetes epidemic
Design diabetes prevention strategies for high-risk populations using evidence-based lifestyle interventions and population-level approaches
Evaluate diabetes care delivery models for different health system contexts, comparing primary care integration approaches across income settings
Assess the cost-effectiveness of diabetes prevention and management interventions, considering health equity implications across populations
Develop comprehensive diabetes management programs integrating technology innovations and chronic disease management principles for resource-constrained settings

key_topics.topic: Type 1, Type 2, and gestational diabetes pathophysiology
Obesity and metabolic syndrome definitions and prevalence
Diabetes risk factors: genetic, lifestyle, environmental
Diabetes prevention strategies and lifestyle interventions
Diabetes management and glycemic control approaches
Diabetes complications: cardiovascular, renal, retinal, neuropathic
Healthcare delivery models for diabetes care
Social determinants and health equity in diabetes care
Economic burden and cost-effectiveness of interventions

case_studies.title: Finnish Diabetes Prevention Programme (DPP)
case_studies.focus: Lifestyle intervention for Type 2 diabetes prevention
key_lessons: Evidence-based lifestyle modification reducing diabetes incidence by 58% in high-risk individuals

case_studies.title: Mexico DIABETIMSS Integrated Care Program
case_studies.focus: Comprehensive diabetes care delivery through social security system
key_lessons: Health system integration improving glycemic control and reducing complications

case_studies.title: India Diabetes Prevention through Lifestyle Modification
case_studies.focus: Community-based diabetes prevention in urban and rural settings
key_lessons: Culturally adapted lifestyle interventions in diverse socioeconomic contexts

case_studies.title: Chile Food Labeling and Childhood Obesity Prevention
case_studies.focus: Population-level intervention for childhood obesity prevention
key_lessons: Policy intervention using warning labels reducing consumption of high-calorie foods among children

items.question: According to the International Diabetes Federation, what was the estimated global prevalence of diabetes among adults aged 20-79 years in 2021?
Which component is NOT part of the metabolic syndrome definition according to the International Diabetes Federation criteria?
The Finnish Diabetes Prevention Programme demonstrated that lifestyle intervention reduced Type 2 diabetes incidence by approximately:
items.learning_objective: Remember evidence from diabetes prevention trials

items.question: Which diabetes management approach has shown the greatest effectiveness in low-resource settings?
options: B) Primary care integration with task-shifting

items.question: The most cost-effective diabetes prevention strategy for population-level implementation is:
options: A) Individual counseling for all adults
B) Screening and treatment of high-risk individuals
D) Intensive lifestyle programs for entire population

items.question: Describe the key components of an effective diabetes prevention program for high-risk populations, including behavioral targets and implementation strategies.
Compare and contrast diabetes care delivery models in high-income versus low- and middle-income countries, discussing barriers and facilitating factors.

Evaluate the role of technology innovations (digital health platforms, continuous glucose monitoring, mobile applications) in improving diabetes management outcomes, considering accessibility and equity issues.

items.title: Designing a National Diabetes Prevention Strategy
items.scenario: You are advising the Ministry of Health in a lower-middle income country with rising diabetes prevalence (current 8.2%) and limited healthcare infrastructure. Design a comprehensive prevention strategy.
questions: What population-level interventions would you prioritize and why?
How would you identify and target high-risk populations?
What delivery mechanisms would be most appropriate given resource constraints?
How would you evaluate the effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of your strategy?

items.title: Technology Integration in Diabetes Care
items.scenario: A healthcare organization wants to integrate digital health technologies into their diabetes management program serving a diverse population with varying levels of digital literacy and access.
questions: What technology solutions would be most appropriate for different population segments?
How would you address digital equity and access barriers?
What training and support systems would be needed?
How would you measure the impact on clinical outcomes and patient satisfaction?

quality_metrics.evidence_level: Primary research sources for all factual claims
quality_metrics.global_representation: Examples and data from all WHO regions
quality_metrics.health_equity: Explicit discussion of disparities and equity considerations
quality_metrics.practical_application: Implementation guidance for different health system contexts
quality_metrics.current_relevance: References primarily from last 10 years, with foundational studies as appropriate
